rag_rules: Fix 'named thing must be an expression with a type' by resolving root identifier references or removing invalid clauses.
Ensure AGREE type declarations use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions have well-defined types and visible identifiers.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in annex expressions.
Align assumed types with actual expression types in AGREE guarantees and assumptions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must declare types explicitly: eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value;
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs without relying on internal implementation details.
Boolean logic in AGREE should use valid operators: and, or, not; expressions must evaluate to bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not assume types without verifying they match the expression's actual type.
Do not generate AGREE code that introduces new package references not declared in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type contracts
